page: Amazon Web Services m7i.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and contains no local storage or dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations place this bare-metal instance in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 460.29 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, drive top-tier results in cryptographic, ray tracing, and text processing benchmarks, alongside strong performance in large language model inference tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.metal-48xl offers high resource density and bare-metal performance without virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for massive parallel workloads despite its premium hardware class. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on external network-attached storage, which is supported by the 50 Gbps network interface. This server is highly suited for enterprise database operations, large-scale software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning or LLM inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors running at 3.2 GHz. Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface, this instance does not include local storage or GPUs. It delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, supported by advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for resource-intensive workloads. It is optimized for enterprise databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
